channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Musk and Trump are viewed roughly the same by Americans, an AP-NORC poll finds
item.description: Elon Musk’s attachment to President-elect Donald Trump has created an unprecedented alliance between America’s most powerful politician and its richest businessman.
item.dc:creator: Thomas Beaumont, Sarah Parvini And Amelia Thomson-Deveaux, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Congress unveils funding deal with more than $100 billion in disaster aid
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Congressional leaders have unveiled a stopgap spending bill that will keep the federal government funded through March 14 and provide more than $100 billion in emergency aid to help states and local communities recover from Hurricanes Helene and Milton and other natural disasters.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Biden says there’s ‘no sense of danger’ from drones
item.description: “Nothing nefarious apparently, but they’re checking it all out,” Biden told reporters about the drones.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Democrats pick Gerry Connolly for Oversight post, rejecting Ocasio-Cortez bid
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — House Democrats on Tuesday picked 74-year-old Rep. Gerry Connolly to lead the party next year atop an influential congressional committee, pushing aside growing calls for generational change in leadership ahead of a second term for Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: FARNOUSH AMIRI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Nevada's 6 Electoral College votes certified for President-elect Donald Trump
item.description: Nevada Republican Party Chairman Michael McDonald officially certified the state's six Electoral College votes for President-elect Donald Trump on Tuesday during a gathering on the UNR campus in Reno.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Lawmakers eye 'low hanging fruit' for government efficiency after first DOGE Caucus meeting
item.description: The Congressional DOGE Caucus had its first meeting on Tuesday, and lawmakers left hopeful about what is to come.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Congress unveils bill to avert Friday government shutdown with over $100B in disaster aid
item.description: House and Senate leaders have agreed on a short-term government funding bill to avert a Friday partial shutdown and kick the fiscal year 2025 funding fight into next year.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fox News Politics: Cheney Accused of 'Potential' Witness Tampering
item.description: The Fox News Politics newsletter, featuring the latest updates on the Trump transition, exclusive interviews and more Fox News politics content.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: GOP senator moves to block feds from disposing of border wall materials amid auction backlash
item.description: Sen. Bill Hagerty, R-Tenn. is introducing legislation to stop the federal government from auctioning border wall materials at the southern border.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pete Hegseth may release sexual assault accuser from confidentiality agreement, setting up public showdown
item.description: Pete Hegseth, Trump’s defense secretary nominee ensnared in sexual assault allegations, plans to release his accuser from the confidentiality agreement he had her sign, according to Sen. Lindsey Graham.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Democrats divided over Biden administration response to reported drone sightings
item.description: The Biden administration’s response to mounting public concern over alleged drone sightings in the Northeast is dividing Democrats on Capitol Hill, where some lawmakers are defending the executive assurances that there’s no threat to public safety and others are bashing the various agencies for not
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: House bill would ban 'unnecessary' genital surgeries for intersex foster youth
item.description: States receiving federal funding for foster care and adoption assistance programs would be required to prohibit certain surgeries for foster children born with intersex traits under legislation introduced Monday by Rep. Sylvia Garcia (D-Texas).
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, NewsNation

item.title: Connolly bests Ocasio-Cortez in key vote to lead Democrats on Oversight panel
item.description: Rep. Gerry Connolly (D-Va.) on Monday claimed the first round of the two-step contest to lead Democrats on the House Oversight and Accountability Committee in the next Congress, besting Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez (D-N.Y.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, NewsNation

item.title: Federal agents, hazmat sent to home of Ohio Secretary of State
item.description: Multiple agencies were sent to Ohio Secretary of State Frank Larose's residence for a reported "security incident.
item.dc:creator: Carlos Mathis, NewsNation

item.title: Government funding bill will run through March, include $100B in disaster aid
item.description: Pieces of a bipartisan effort to prevent a government shutdown during the holiday season are beginning to come into focus, including a March end date and $100 billion in disaster aid. GOP leadership briefed Republicans on Tuesday morning on some of the details of the forthcoming legislation.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: In disputed Shakopee-area legislative race, voters share what was on uncounted ballots
item.description: While 20 ballots believed to have been discarded before being counted won’t be recovered, some voters who cast those missing ballots shared what was on them. They appear to show Rep. Brad Tabke’s narrow lead would stand up.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Trump sues Des Moines Register, pollster
item.description: “Selzer’s polling ‘miss’ was not an astonishing coincidence — it was intentional,” the complaint states.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Americans end 2024 with grim economic outlook, but Republicans are optimistic for 2025: AP-NORC poll
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The unemployment rate is healthy and the stock market is up, but Democrats are feeling more pessimistic about the U.S. economy after Donald Trump's election victory, according to a new poll.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK and LINLEY SANDERS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Freedom Caucus reelects Andy Harris as chair
item.description: Rep. Andy Harris (R-Md.) will chair the House Freedom Caucus through the end of 2025, members of the group decided Monday night, cementing his leadership of the hard-line conservative caucus.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge rejects Trump’s bid to toss hush money conviction
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A judge Monday refused to throw out President-elect Donald Trump’s hush money conviction because of the U.S. Supreme Court's recent ruling on presidential immunity. But the overall future of the historic case remains unclear. Manhattan Judge Juan M.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: RFK Jr. meets with senators as questions swirl about Trump's pick to lead health agency
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Robert F. Kennedy Jr. swept onto Capitol Hill late Monday as the anti-vaccine health guru from the famous political family reintroduced himself to senators, this time as President-elect Donald Trump's pick to lead the nation's Health and Human Services Department.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO and MARY CLARE JALONICK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate Democrats push plan to abolish Electoral College
item.description: Three Democratic senators unveiled a constitutional amendment to abolish the Electoral College system Monday, just more than a month after President-elect Trump stunned the Democrats by sweeping all seven battleground states, knocking off three Senate Democratic incumbents in the process. Sens.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Journalists anticipate a renewed hostility toward their work under the incoming Trump administration
item.description: For the press heading into a second Trump administration, there's a balancing act between being prepared and being fearful.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Speaker Johnson faces 'complicated' decision on crucial Rules Committee chair
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) is barreling into a tricky — yet critical — decision over whom to appoint as the next chair of the House Rules Committee, a post that will set the tone for legislating and play a key role in keeping Republicans united in next year’s ultrathin majority.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, Fox 5

item.title: Trial over Minnesota House race that could swing control of chamber focuses on lost ballots
item.description: Republicans challenged the election result arguing that a 14-vote margin between the DFL representative who won and his GOP opponent could be made up in a pool of 20 ballots that were accidentally tossed.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Pelosi 'on the mend' after hip replacement surgery in Germany
item.description: "Speaker Pelosi is enjoying the overwhelming outpouring of prayers and well wishes," Ian Krager said.
item.dc:creator: Steff Danielle Thomas, NewsNation

item.title: Trump leans on czars for key policy areas
item.description: Trump plans to lean on appointed czars to carry out his plans in key policy areas, specifically when it comes to immigration and energy production.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Biden urges Congress to 'do something' for gun reform on Sandy Hook anniversary
item.description: Biden in a press release touted his administration's efforts to combat gun violence but argued that there was more to be done — especially as his presidency enters the final stretch.
item.dc:creator: Steff Danielle Thomas, NewsNation

item.title: Lombardo joins GOP governors 'fully committed' to Trump deportation threats
item.description: Gov. Joe Lombardo and 25 other Republican governors lined up in support of President-elect Donald Trump's immigration policies on Wednesday, calling for the deportation of "individuals who pose a threat.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: FBI Director Wray to resign at end of Biden administration
item.description: FBI Director Christopher Wray on Wednesday announced he would resign from the bureau at the end of the Biden administration next month, with more than two years remaining on his term in office.
item.dc:creator: Carrie Johnson and Ryan Lucas, MPR News
